summary: South African Nobel Prize-winning novelist. Known for The Conservationist, Burger's Daughter, July's People, A Guest of Honour, and The Late Bourgeois World. Wrote extensively about apartheid.

works.name: The Conservationist
works.description: Novel about a pig-iron businessman whose farm harbors the body of a mysterious man.

clues.clue: Pig-iron businessman Mehring is appalled when a mysterious man's body is dumped on his farm; body unearthed by a flood
examples: A pig-iron businessman is appalled when the body of a mysterious man is dumped in one of these places before being unearthed by a flood

works.name: Burger's Daughter
works.description: Novel about Rosa Burger, daughter of an anti-apartheid activist.

clues.clue: Rosa Burger is the daughter of a Lionel Burger, an anti-apartheid activist modeled on Bram Fischer
examples: the daughter of an anti-apartheid activist

works.name: July's People
works.description: Novel about a white South African family fleeing to their servant's village during an uprising.

clues.clue: The Smales family flees to their servant July's village during a black uprising
examples: a white family takes refuge in their servant's village

works.name: A Guest of Honour
works.description: Novel about Evelyn James Bray returning to a fictional African country after independence.

clues.clue: Mweta invites Bray back after independence; Bray contacts Edward Shinza
examples: the Gala speaker Mweta inviting Evelyn James Bray back to his country after gaining independence

works.name: General
works.description: South African Nobel laureate; apartheid themes.

clues.clue: South African Nobel Prize-winning author; wrote extensively about apartheid

comprehensive_summary: Nadine Gordimer (1923-2014) was a South African Nobel Prize-winning novelist who wrote extensively about apartheid. The Conservationist follows a pig-iron businessman whose farm harbors a mysterious body unearthed by flood. Burger's Daughter follows Rosa Burger, daughter of an anti-apartheid activist modeled on Bram Fischer. July's People depicts a white family fleeing to their servant's village during an uprising. A Guest of Honour follows Bray returning to a post-independence African country.

cards.front: Novel: a pig-iron businessman is appalled when a mysterious body is dumped on his farm; unearthed by a flood
Novel: Rosa Burger is the daughter of an anti-apartheid activist modeled on Bram Fischer
Novel: the white Smales family flees to their servant July's village during a black uprising
